items.title: Immune–inflammatory vulnerability index for risk stratification in older adults with acute exacerbations of COPD: a prospective cohort study | BMC Geriatrics | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: A prospective cohort study proposed an immune-inflammatory vulnerability index for risk stratification in older adults with acute exacerbations of COPD.
items.curatedReason: 这项前瞻性队列研究提出了针对急性加重期 COPD 老年患者的免疫炎症脆弱性指数，用于风险分层。它为你在评估和管理 COPD 老年患者时提供了新的风险预测工具。
items.titleZh: 免疫炎症脆弱性指数用于急性加重期 COPD 老年患者的风险分层：一项前瞻性队列研究。
items.summaryZh: 一项前瞻性队列研究提出了免疫炎症脆弱性指数，用于急性加重期慢性阻塞性肺疾病（COPD）老年患者的风险分层。
items.curatedReasonEn: This prospective cohort study proposed an immune-inflammatory vulnerability index for risk stratification in older adults with acute exacerbations of COPD. It provides you with a new risk prediction tool for assessing and managing elderly COPD patients.
items.limitation: 队列研究，可能存在未测量的混杂因素影响结果。
items.limitationEn: Cohort study, potential for unmeasured confounding factors affecting results.

items.title: Advances in research on the role of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke - ScienceDirect
items.summary: This review summarizes advances in research on the dual roles of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke, highlighting their involvement in promoting angiogenesis and neuroprotection.
items.curatedReason: 这篇综述探讨了 VEGF 家族成员在缺血性卒中中的双重作用，包括促血管生成和神经保护。它提供了一个生物学机制的视角，但对 PT 临床实践的直接指导有限。
items.titleZh: VEGF 家族成员在缺血性卒中中作用的研究进展。
items.summaryZh: 这篇综述总结了 VEGF 家族成员在缺血性卒中中双重作用的研究进展，强调其在促进血管生成和神经保护方面的参与。
items.curatedReasonEn: This review discusses the dual roles of VEGF family members in ischemic stroke, including angiogenesis promotion and neuroprotection. It offers a biological mechanism perspective, but its direct guidance for PT clinical practice is limited.
items.limitation: 这是一篇机制综述，未提供具体的临床干预证据。
items.limitationEn: This is a review on mechanisms, not providing specific clinical intervention evidence.

items.title: Data analysis tool for identifying multidimensional health profiles associated with frailty in older adults | BMC Medical Informatics and Decision Making | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: This study describes a data analysis tool for identifying multidimensional health profiles associated with frailty in older adults.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究开发了一个数据分析工具，用于识别与老年人虚弱相关的多维度健康状况。它为你在评估老年虚弱患者时，提供了一个更全面的视角和潜在的辅助工具。
items.titleZh: 用于识别与老年人虚弱相关的多维度健康状况的数据分析工具。
items.summaryZh: 本研究介绍了一种数据分析工具，用于识别与老年人虚弱相关的多维度健康状况。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study developed a data analysis tool to identify multidimensional health profiles associated with frailty in older adults. It provides you with a more comprehensive perspective and a potential assistive tool when assessing frail older patients.
items.limitation: 这是一个数据分析工具的描述，并非临床干预研究。
items.limitationEn: This is a description of a data analysis tool, not a clinical intervention study.

items.title: Running exercise alleviates chronic heart failure by promoting cardiomyocyte autophagic flux through the NEAT1–QKI affecting Beclin1/LC3B mRNA stability | Biology Direct | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: This research demonstrated that running exercise alleviates chronic heart failure in animal models by promoting cardiomyocyte autophagic flux through the NEAT1–QKI pathway, affecting Beclin1/LC3B mRNA stability.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究在动物模型中探讨了跑步运动通过特定分子通路缓解慢性心力衰竭的机制。这是一个有前景的生物学发现，但目前仍是基础研究，不直接影响 PT 临床实践。
items.titleZh: 跑步运动通过促进 NEAT1–QKI 影响 Beclin1/LC3B mRNA 稳定性来缓解慢性心力衰竭。
items.summaryZh: 本研究表明，跑步运动通过促进 NEAT1–QKI 影响 Beclin1/LC3B mRNA 稳定性，从而缓解慢性心力衰竭，该发现基于动物模型。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study explored the mechanism by which running exercise alleviates chronic heart failure through specific molecular pathways in animal models. This is a promising biological discovery, but it remains basic research at present and does not directly impact PT clinical practice.
items.limitation: 动物模型研究，结果不一定能直接推导至人类。
items.limitationEn: Animal model study, results may not directly translate to humans.

items.title: Relationship between heart failure and the risk of acute exacerbation of COPD
items.summary: This original research investigates the relationship between heart failure and the risk of acute exacerbation of COPD, highlighting comorbidities' impact on prognosis.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究探讨了心力衰竭与 COPD 急性加重风险之间的关系。它提醒你关注心肺共病对患者预后的影响，但结论尚需更多高级别证据来指导临床决策。
items.titleZh: 心力衰竭与慢性阻塞性肺疾病急性加重风险的关系。
items.summaryZh: 这项原创研究探讨了心力衰竭与慢性阻塞性肺疾病（COPD）急性加重风险之间的关系，强调了合并症对预后的影响。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study investigates the relationship between heart failure and the risk of acute exacerbation of COPD. It reminds you to pay attention to the impact of cardiopulmonary comorbidities on patient prognosis, but more high-level evidence is needed to guide clinical decision-making.
items.limitation: 这是一项回顾性观察研究，可能存在混杂因素。
items.limitationEn: This is a retrospective observational study, which may be subject to confounding factors.

items.title: A preliminary analysis of the inflammatory protein landscape in the CSF of mid- to late-stage Parkinson’s disease: associations with motor severity and subtypes | BMC Neurology | Springer Nature Link
items.summary: A preliminary analysis characterized the inflammatory protein landscape in the CSF of mid- to late-stage Parkinson’s disease patients and its associations with motor severity and subtypes.
items.curatedReason: 这项初步分析揭示了中晚期帕金森病脑脊液中炎症蛋白的图谱及其与运动严重程度和亚型的关联。这有助于你理解帕金森病的病理生理学，但其临床转化仍需时间。
items.titleZh: 中晚期帕金森病脑脊液炎症蛋白图谱的初步分析：与运动严重程度和亚型的关联。
items.summaryZh: 一项初步分析揭示了中晚期帕金森病患者脑脊液中炎症蛋白的图谱，及其与运动严重程度和亚型的关联。
items.curatedReasonEn: This preliminary analysis revealed the inflammatory protein landscape in CSF of mid- to late-stage Parkinson's disease and its associations with motor severity and subtypes. This helps you understand Parkinson's pathophysiology, but clinical translation is still awaited.
items.limitation: 初步分析，样本量和研究设计可能限制结论的普遍性。
items.limitationEn: Preliminary analysis, sample size and study design may limit generalizability of conclusions.

items.title: Cannabidiol alleviates traumatic brain injury-induced neuronal damage and cognitive deficits by inhibiting ferroptosis via the TRPV1/MCU/PI3K/Akt pathway
items.summary: Cannabidiol (CBD) alleviates traumatic brain injury-induced neuronal damage and cognitive deficits by inhibiting ferroptosis via the TRPV1/MCU/PI3K/Akt pathway in an in vitro study.
items.curatedReason: 这项研究探讨了大麻二酚通过抑制铁死亡来缓解创伤性脑损伤引起的神经元损伤和认知缺陷的机制。这是一个有前景的生物学方向，但目前仍处于基础研究阶段，不直接影响 PT 临床实践。
items.titleZh: 大麻二酚通过抑制 TRPV1/MCU/PI3K/Akt 通路中的铁死亡来缓解创伤性脑损伤引起的神经元损伤和认知缺陷。
items.summaryZh: 一项体外研究表明，大麻二酚（CBD）通过抑制 TRPV1/MCU/PI3K/Akt 通路中的铁死亡，减轻创伤性脑损伤引起的神经元损伤和认知缺陷。
items.curatedReasonEn: This study explores the mechanism by which cannabidiol alleviates TBI-induced neuronal damage and cognitive deficits by inhibiting ferroptosis. This is a promising biological direction, but it is currently at a basic research stage and does not directly impact PT clinical practice.
items.limitation: 动物模型和机制研究，尚无法直接推导临床干预。
items.limitationEn: Animal model and mechanistic study, not directly translatable to clinical intervention yet.
